text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
RESIDENT/FELLOW: [REDACTED]

SPECIMEN DESIGNATION:
  A: Right kidney with tumor, right renal vein segment, and perirenal fat
  B: Right adrenal gland (en bloc)
  C: Retroperitoneal lymph nodes (right paracaval, interaortocaval, and right common iliac template)

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Right renal mass, stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, with macroscopic renal vein tumor thrombus, retroperitoneal lymph node metastasis, and bilateral pulmonary metastases.

PROCEDURE: Right radical nephrectomy with en bloc right adrenalectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (cytoreductive intent). Open approach via right flank incision.

CLINICAL HISTORY: 63-year-old female with biopsy-proven clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 4 with 20% sarcomatoid differentiation. Known pulmonary metastases (bilateral). IMDC intermediate risk. Tumor genomic profiling (Foundation One CDx equivalent) previously performed on core biopsy specimen demonstrating VHL p.R200* loss-of-function mutation (homozygous), PBRM1 p.Q1233* mutation, SETD2 frameshift deletion, TP53 p.R175H, and MET high-level amplification. PD-L1 CPS 12 by 22C3 assay. Cytoreductive surgery performed prior to planned systemic immunotherapy.

---

FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSES:

(A) RIGHT KIDNEY, RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY SPECIMEN:

   CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, FUHRMAN NUCLEAR GRADE 4
   - Tumor size: 14.2 x 11.6 x 10.1 cm (greatest dimension 14.2 cm)
   - Sarcomatoid differentiation: PRESENT, comprising approximately 20% of total tumor volume
   - Rhabdoid differentiation: Absent
   - Tumor necrosis: Present, extensive (estimated >50% of tumor volume)
   - Pathologic T stage: pT3b
     - Macroscopic venous extension: PRESENT - tumor thrombus extends into the right renal vein proper (beyond the renal sinus veins), completely resected
     - Renal sinus fat invasion: Present
     - Perirenal fat invasion: Present (extension into perirenal fat, within Gerota's fascia)
     - Adrenal gland involvement: Absent (see specimen B)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: Present (beyond the main tumor thrombus; small vessel LVI identified in peritumoral tissue)
   - Surgical margins:
     - Renal vein surgical margin: NEGATIVE (tumor thrombus present but margin of resection free of tumor)
     - Ureteral margin: Negative (uninvolved)
     - Soft tissue/Gerota fascia margin: Negative (R0 resection)
     - Adrenal margin (see specimen B): Negative
   - Non-neoplastic kidney: Moderate nephrosclerosis consistent with hypertensive nephropathy. Focal diabetic glomerulosclerosis (mild). No additional primary renal pathology identified.

(B) RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND (en bloc resection):
   - NO METASTATIC CARCINOMA
   - Fibrous adhesion to the upper pole of the tumor noted grossly; histologically, this represents reactive fibrosis with focal chronic inflammation without direct tumor invasion
   - Adrenal gland with mild cortical nodularity (incidental)

(C) RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODES (right paracaval, interaortocaval, right common iliac template):
   - 1 OF 12 LYMPH NODES POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA
   - Largest positive node: 2.4 cm, right hilar/paracaval station
     - Extranodal extension: PRESENT
   - 11 of 12 lymph nodes: Negative for malignancy
   - Pathologic N stage: pN1

OVERALL PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th Edition): pT3b pN1 M1 - Stage IV

---

GROSS PATHOLOGIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received fresh, then placed in formalin. The specimen consists of a right kidney with attached perirenal fat and a segment of right renal vein. The kidney measures 17.0 x 13.5 x 11.2 cm and weighs 1,134 grams (after fixation). The external surface of the perirenal fat is smooth and tan-yellow. Serial sectioning reveals a large, heterogeneous mass occupying the upper and mid pole of the kidney, measuring 14.2 x 11.6 x 10.1 cm. The mass has a variegated cut surface with alternating golden-yellow, tan-white, and gray-white areas. Extensive central necrosis is present (estimated 50-60% of the tumor volume), with hemorrhagic and cystic foci. Focal calcification is identified. The tumor abuts and infiltrates the renal sinus fat. The tumor extends into the renal vein, where a firm, tan-gray intraluminal tumor thrombus measuring 3.8 x 1.2 cm is identified. The renal vein margin of resection is represented and sampled. The perirenal fat is diffusely infiltrated by tumor at the upper pole but the Gerota fascia margin appears grossly intact. The renal pelvis and upper ureter are free of tumor. The ureter is sampled at the surgical margin. Residual non-neoplastic renal parenchyma is identified at the lower pole, grossly unremarkable. Representative sections of normal kidney submitted.

Specimen B: The right adrenal gland is received en bloc with the kidney specimen, firmly adherent to the upper pole tumor by a dense fibrous band. After separation, the adrenal gland measures 4.8 x 2.5 x 0.8 cm and weighs 11 grams. The external surface is smooth, tan-yellow. Sectioning reveals a normal-appearing cortex and medulla without discrete nodule greater than 0.5 cm. The cortex has a uniform yellow color. No gross tumor involvement identified. The fibrous adhesion site is submitted in its entirety for histologic evaluation.

Specimen C: Received in multiple labeled containers corresponding to nodal stations:
  - Right paracaval nodes: Multiple fibrofatty fragments containing 6 lymph nodes, largest measuring 2.4 cm (grossly replaced by gray-white firm tumor tissue, 2.4 x 2.1 cm area of gross metastasis with apparent breach of the capsule)
  - Interaortocaval nodes: Multiple fibrofatty fragments containing 4 lymph nodes, largest 1.0 cm, all grossly unremarkable
  - Right common iliac nodes: Multiple fibrofatty fragments containing 2 lymph nodes, both grossly unremarkable (largest 0.8 cm)
  Total lymph nodes submitted: 12

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A - Primary Tumor:
The tumor is composed of two morphologically distinct components:

1. Clear Cell Component (approximately 80% of viable tumor): Nests, alveoli, and sheets of polygonal cells with abundant clear, vacuolated cytoplasm and well-defined cell membranes consistent with lipid/glycogen content. The nuclei are enlarged, pleomorphic, and irregular with prominent eosinophilic nucleoli readily visible at low power (Fuhrman grade 4 features; equivalent to WHO/ISUP grade 4). Nuclear membrane irregularities and occasional bizarre nuclear forms are identified. Mitotic figures are frequent (up to 6 per 10 high power fields, including atypical mitoses). The stroma is characteristically thin-walled, richly vascularized (delicate sinusoidal vasculature). Focal areas of rhabdoid-like cytology are absent. Background clear cell areas show prominent hemorrhage and hemosiderin deposition. Geographic tumor necrosis is extensive.

2. Sarcomatoid Component (approximately 20% of viable tumor): Interspersed with and at the periphery of the clear cell component, there is transition to a population of overtly sarcomatoid cells characterized by spindled to pleomorphic morphology, discohesive growth pattern, storiform and fascicular arrangements, high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, and brisk mitotic activity (up to 18 mitoses per 10 high power fields, including atypical forms). These sarcomatoid areas show geographic necrosis. The interface between the clear cell and sarcomatoid zones shows gradual transition without an abrupt demarcation, supporting dedifferentiation of the primary carcinoma. No heterologous sarcomatoid elements (osteosarcomatous, chondrosarcomatous, or rhabdomyosarcomatous differentiation) are identified.

Renal Sinus and Vascular Invasion: The tumor extensively infiltrates the renal sinus fat. Macroscopic tumor thrombus is confirmed histologically within the right renal vein lumen, composed predominantly of the clear cell carcinoma component with focal sarcomatoid areas. Small vessel lymphovascular invasion is identified in the peritumoral tissue beyond the main thrombus. The renal vein surgical margin is negative; there is a 0.8 cm clearance from the leading edge of the tumor thrombus to the inked margin.

Perirenal Fat: Tumor infiltration into perirenal fat confirmed histologically at multiple sections. The Gerota fascia is intact and the soft tissue margin is negative (distance from tumor to closest soft tissue margin: 1.2 mm).

Non-neoplastic Kidney: The residual renal parenchyma shows arteriolosclerosis and arteriolar hyalinosis consistent with hypertensive nephropathy. Focal global glomerulosclerosis (approximately 15% of glomeruli) and mild diabetic glomerulosclerosis (mild mesangial expansion, no Kimmelstiel-Wilson nodules) are noted. Tubular atrophy and interstitial fibrosis are moderate.

Specimen B - Right Adrenal Gland:
The adrenal gland shows no metastatic tumor. The fibrous adhesion zone demonstrates reactive fibrosis, focal fat necrosis, and mild chronic inflammation. No direct tumor invasion of the adrenal parenchyma is identified. Incidental mild cortical nodular hyperplasia noted.

Specimen C - Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes:
The right paracaval/hilar node (2.4 cm) is extensively replaced by metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, showing the same morphologic features as the primary tumor (clear cell and focal sarcomatoid components). Extranodal extension is confirmed histologically with tumor penetrating through the nodal capsule into perinodal adipose tissue over a broad front (greatest extent of extranodal extension: approximately 5 mm). All 11 remaining lymph nodes from the interaortocaval and right common iliac stations show reactive lymphoid hyperplasia without metastatic involvement.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

Immunohistochemistry (performed on sections of the primary tumor, confirmatory panel):

  Carbonic Anhydrase IX (CA9 / CAIX):  POSITIVE - diffuse, strong membranous and cytoplasmic staining in >95% of clear cell component; focal/patchy staining in sarcomatoid areas (consistent with dedifferentiation)
  PAX8:                                POSITIVE - diffuse nuclear staining in clear cell component; retained (focal) in sarcomatoid component
  RCC Antigen (CD10):                  POSITIVE - membranous/cytoplasmic staining in clear cell component
  Vimentin:                            POSITIVE - strong and diffuse in sarcomatoid component; patchy in clear cell component
  CK8/18 (MNF116):                     POSITIVE (focal) - predominantly in clear cell component; largely negative in sarcomatoid areas
  TFE3:                                NEGATIVE (excludes Xp11 translocation RCC)
  TFEB:                                NEGATIVE
  CD117 (c-KIT):                       NEGATIVE (excludes chromophobe RCC)
  CK7:                                 NEGATIVE in clear cell component; focal weak in sarcomatoid component
  HMGA2:                               POSITIVE (focal) in sarcomatoid areas
  SOX10:                               NEGATIVE (excludes melanocytic differentiation in sarcomatoid component)
  Desmin:                              NEGATIVE (no rhabdomyosarcomatous differentiation)
  SMA (smooth muscle actin):           FOCAL POSITIVE in sarcomatoid component (non-specific)

PD-L1 Expression (22C3 pharmDx antibody, Dako/Agilent platform):
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 12
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 8%
  Immune cell staining: Present in tumor-infiltrating mononuclear cells, predominantly at the sarcomatoid/clear cell interface and in areas of necrosis
  Interpretation: PD-L1 CPS 12 (positive per threshold >=1)

Ki-67 Proliferative Index:
  Clear cell component: Approximately 12% (range across sections: 8-15%)
  Sarcomatoid component: Approximately 35-40% (range across sections: 30-42%)

MLH1 / MSH2 / MSH6 / PMS2 (Mismatch Repair Proteins, IHC screen):
  All four proteins: INTACT nuclear expression (no loss)
  Interpretation: Proficient mismatch repair (pMMR) by IHC, consistent with prior molecular MSS determination on NGS

Note: Molecular genomic profiling (Foundation One CDx equivalent panel) was previously performed on the preoperative core biopsy specimen and is reported separately. Confirmatory IHC results from the nephrectomy specimen are consistent with those findings.

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING SUMMARY (AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition):

  Primary Tumor (pT):        pT3b - Tumor extends into the renal vein with macroscopic venous tumor thrombus
  Regional Lymph Nodes (pN): pN1 - Metastasis in one regional lymph node (1/12 nodes positive; extranodal extension present)
  Distant Metastases (pM):   pM1 - Known bilateral pulmonary metastases (established clinically/radiologically; not present in this specimen)
  Histologic Grade:          Fuhrman Grade 4 / WHO/ISUP Grade 4
  Histologic Type:           Clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%)
  Venous Invasion:           Present (macroscopic - renal vein; microscopic - small vessel LVI)
  Perineural Invasion:       Not identified
  Lymphatic Invasion:        Present
  Tumor Necrosis:            Present (extensive)
  Surgical Margins:          R0 (all margins negative)
  Additional Pathologic Findings: Adrenal gland negative for metastatic carcinoma; extranodal extension in positive lymph node

Overall Pathologic Stage: pT3b pN1 M1 -- Stage IV (AJCC 8th Ed.)

---

PATHOLOGIST'S NOTE:

The morphologic and immunophenotypic findings in this nephrectomy specimen are consistent with the prior core needle biopsy diagnosis. The clear cell renal cell carcinoma demonstrates high-grade (Fuhrman/WHO-ISUP grade 4) nuclear features throughout with a significant sarcomatoid component (approximately 20%), a well-established adverse prognostic indicator in ccRCC associated with inferior outcomes on standard VEGF-targeted therapies and potential enhanced sensitivity to immune checkpoint inhibition. The extensive tumor necrosis (>50%) is an additional adverse prognostic finding.

The MET amplification detected on prior NGS (copy number 8.2) is particularly noteworthy in the context of sarcomatoid differentiation, as MET pathway activation has been associated with epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition and acquisition of sarcomatoid morphology in renal cell carcinoma. This finding may be relevant for therapeutic decision-making with MET/VEGFR2 inhibitors.

The R0 resection with complete removal of the renal vein tumor thrombus is confirmed on histologic examination. The close soft tissue margin (1.2 mm) is noted but deemed negative.

Specimen adequacy: Adequate for pathologic evaluation. Representative sections from all submitted specimens examined.

Electronic signature: Attending Pathologist, MD, PhD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Academic Medical Center
